rationale.R1.1: A generic section review centrally includes checking whether the thesis and contribution framing are coherent, especially given the shift from DialogueDev to RSP.
rationale.R1.2: Reviewing the section entails assessing whether the argumentative chain from protocol description to prompting-level contribution is internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims from benchmark and issue-resolution results, so checking whether those results support the stated conclusions is central.
rationale.R1.5: The section-level narrative arc is implicated because the artifact appears to introduce one system and then reframe the central contribution around another mechanism.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty about what kind of review was intended by the brief delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The projected responsibilities should avoid implying duties beyond what a section review reasonably covers.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection only and not perform the requested review itself.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid downstream harm from overstating unsupported review obligations or encouraging inappropriate action on the artifact.
rationale.RX.5: The rationale should trace each active responsibility to the delegation and artifact features.
